qas.q: what does PACT stand for?
What should an exposure plan contain
qas.a: Job classifications with high probability of exposure; A list of tasks are associated with exposure; How to comply with the plan; Post exposure prophylaxis plan

qas.q: What biologic substances could contain blood and bloodborne pathogens?
what are universal procautions to prevent injury
qas.a: treat all biologic liquids as if they are hazardous; do not eat, drink, or smoke in any area that may contain blood or blood-containing material

qas.q: what protective clothing should you wear?
qas.a: shoe covers, goggles, face shield, vinyl gloves, gown, apron, lab coat, cap, hood

qas.q: what should you do with open cuts when working with potential sources of blood
qas.a: keep cuts, abrasions, mucous membranes and areas of dermatitis covered

qas.q: if you have a reasonable chance of coming into contact with blood or blood-containing materials, what vaccine can you request
how often is BBP training reviewed
what is the most common scenario for health care personnel acquiring a blood borne pathogen?
qas.a: getting stuck by a sharp object

qas.q: should you ever recap a needle in a health care environment?
where and how should you place needles, if no special protective needle sheath is included, once you are done using them?
qas.a: place the needle in sharps without cap

qas.q: how do you prevent others from becoming exposed
examples of initial exposure materials
How do you deal with exposure to BBP?
qas.a: remove protective gloves properly, wash hands well after removing gloves, rinse mouth with water if necessary, irrigate your eyes if necessary, tell your supervisor what has happened

qas.q: what is the most effective method of treating a bloodborne pathogen
qas.a: washing the area with soap and water

qas.q: what do you use to wipe up large amounts of blood
what do you use on contaminated surfaces?
where do you dispose of cleaning materials and PPE
what should you always do before cleaning
when you need to tell someone, what form do you fill out?
what is the first priority of exposure to BBP
qas.a: minimize your chance of infection by washing with soap and water, clean wound with antiseptic

qas.q: what is the second priority of exposure to BBP
what is the third priority of exposure to BBP
qas.a: determine need for post exposure prophylaxis and contact your medical provider
